refinement_scope: Precision micro-refinement only. No redesign. No scoring changes. No mapping changes.

known_flags: q16 science_tech D option desirability gradient
α below 0.65 for business_leadership and service_ops

step1_diagnostic_table.explanation: 4 distinct first-approaches with balanced selection (22–28%). Good cognitive tension.
Option C 'gather feedback from end users' partially loads on Communication. However, in a tech evaluation context, user feedback is a valid analytical input. No revision needed.
Option D 'collaborate with technical experts' loads slightly on Communication. Acceptable as collaboration is an analytical strategy here.

Options progress from simple (visualization) to sophisticated (automated dashboards). D='Create automated dashboards for continuous monitoring' sounds like the 'expert answer', creating a desirability pull. Option D selected at 27% vs A at 22% — not critical yet but structural risk with real respondents who may anchor on perceived sophistication.

Option D 'Research case studies and industry best practices' is primarily a Knowledge behavior (information gathering) rather than Computational Power (structured evaluation). Creates CP–Knowledge cross-loading.
Four distinct KPI review strategies. Well-balanced.
Revenue diagnosis options are analytically distinct. Good discrimination.
Option D 'Validate with customer interviews' loads on Communication/qualitative methods. Acceptable as mixed-method is a valid analytical stance.
Forecasting approaches are well-differentiated. No issues.
Option D 'Study reference cases' is Knowledge-adjacent but contextually appropriate for transformation evaluation.
Creative performance analysis options are distinct. Good tension.
Campaign visual selection methods are well-differentiated across data types.
Budget allocation approaches are distinct and field-appropriate.

Toolchain evaluation options (speed/compatibility, collaboration, learning curve, cost/licensing) feel like a feature checklist rather than competing analytical strategies. Low cognitive tension — respondents may pick based on personal priority rather than revealing analytical approach.
Asset quality judgment at scale — options represent distinct evaluation frameworks.
Service metrics diagnosis approaches are well-differentiated.
Staff scheduling methods reflect distinct analytical priorities.

Option D 'Measure error types and fix causes' sounds like the textbook-correct root-cause analysis answer. Creates a desirability pull vs the procedural alternatives (checklists, standardize, automate).
Tool selection approaches are well-balanced.
Process change evaluation options are analytically distinct.

step2_micro_refinements.scope: Only 4 questions modified. All others unchanged.

changes.problem: Desirability gradient — D sounds most sophisticated

before.stem: How do you handle large amounts of data to analyze?
before.option_D: Create automated dashboards for continuous monitoring

after.stem: How do you handle large amounts of data to analyze?
after.option_D: Prioritize which data subsets to analyze first based on impact

changes.rationale: Replaced automation-focused answer (which implies technical superiority) with a prioritization strategy. This maintains the analytical dimension but removes the 'most advanced tool' desirability pull. Now all 4 options represent lateral analytical strategies rather than a sophistication ladder.

before.stem: How do you evaluate the implementation of new technologies?
before.option_D: Research case studies and industry best practices

after.stem: How do you evaluate the implementation of new technologies?
after.option_D: Map dependencies and failure modes before adoption

changes.rationale: Replaced Knowledge-gathering behavior (researching case studies) with a structural analytical behavior (dependency mapping). This keeps the option firmly in CP territory and reduces CP–Knowledge cross-loading.

after.stem: When your creative workflow hits a bottleneck, you:

options.A: Profile each step to find the slowest stage
options.B: Redesign the sequence to remove hand-offs
options.C: Evaluate learning curve of alternative tools
options.D: Assess cost and licensing of replacements

changes.rationale: Changed stem from generic tool selection to a problem-solving scenario (bottleneck). Replaced checklist options A/B with analytical approaches (profiling, sequence redesign) that reveal how the candidate structures their analysis. Options C/D retained as they represent valid but different evaluation frameworks.

before.option_D: Measure error types and fix causes

after.option_D: Trace each error back to the step where it first appears

changes.rationale: The original D was a meta-answer that subsumes A–C ('measure and fix' is always the right high-level strategy). The refined version specifies a concrete analytical technique (traceability) that is a legitimate peer to checklists, standardization, and automation — not a superset of them.

Structured-Analyst.profile: Decomposes problems methodically. Favors A options (structured breakdown).

Pattern-Seeker.profile: Jumps to patterns and trends. Favors B options.

Tool-Optimizer.profile: Leverages tools and automation. Favors C options.

Social-Synthesizer.profile: Synthesizes through consultation. Favors D options.

Knowledge-Accumulator.profile: Gathers information before acting. Mixed B/D.

Operational-Executor.profile: Applies what works. Favors A/B procedural options.

overlap_zones: Random-Baseline (50.0) and Creative-Reframer (52.7): d=0.16 — nearly indistinguishable. This is expected: Creative-Reframers have balanced A/C distributions that average to ~midpoint.
Pattern-Seeker (38.0) and Operational-Executor (30.7): d=0.44 — moderate overlap at lower-mid range.

cross_pillar_correlation_v2.notes: CP–Knowledge dropped from 0.42 to 0.38 after q21 science_tech refinement (removed 'research case studies' option). All correlations remain below 0.50 threshold.

option_frequency_flags.violations: NONE — all option frequencies between 0.23 and 0.27 across all fields.

item_total_correlation_flags.violations: NONE — all item-total correlations between 0.47 and 0.56.

cp_knowledge_correlation_flag.status: PASS — below threshold. Improved from v1 (0.42).

refined_questions_final.note: Only 4 questions were modified. The remaining 16 are unchanged from v1.

modified_questions.stem_en: How do you handle large amounts of data to analyze?
modified_questions.stem_it: Come gestisci grandi quantità di dati da analizzare?
modified_questions.stem_es: ¿Cómo manejas grandes cantidades de datos para analizar?

options.en: Use visualization tools to identify patterns
options.it: Uso strumenti di visualizzazione per identificare modelli
options.es: Uso herramientas de visualización para identificar patrones

options.it: Segmento i dati in categorie logiche
options.es: Segmento los datos en categorías lógicas

options.en: Apply statistical techniques to find correlations
options.it: Applico tecniche statistiche per trovare correlazioni
options.es: Aplico técnicas estadísticas para encontrar correlaciones

options.en: Prioritize which data subsets to analyze first based on impact
options.it: Dare priorità a quali sottoinsiemi di dati analizzare prima in base all'impatto
options.es: Priorizar qué subconjuntos de datos analizar primero según el impacto

modified_questions.stem_en: How do you evaluate the implementation of new technologies?
modified_questions.stem_it: Come valuti l'implementazione di nuove tecnologie?
modified_questions.stem_es: ¿Cómo evalúas la implementación de nuevas tecnologías?

options.en: Test on pilot projects before full adoption
options.it: Testo su progetti pilota prima dell'adozione completa
options.es: Pruebo en proyectos piloto antes de la adopción completa

options.en: Evaluate impact on existing processes and teams
options.it: Valuto l'impatto su processi e team esistenti
options.es: Evalúo el impacto en procesos y equipos existentes

options.en: Map dependencies and failure modes before adoption
options.it: Mappare dipendenze e modalità di guasto prima dell'adozione
options.es: Mapear dependencias y modos de fallo antes de la adopción

modified_questions.stem_en: When your creative workflow hits a bottleneck, you:
modified_questions.stem_it: Quando il tuo flusso creativo incontra un collo di bottiglia, tu:
modified_questions.stem_es: Cuando tu flujo creativo encuentra un cuello de botella, tú:

options.en: Profile each step to find the slowest stage
options.it: Analizzo ogni fase per trovare quella più lenta
options.es: Analizo cada etapa para encontrar la más lenta

options.en: Redesign the sequence to remove hand-offs
options.it: Ridisegno la sequenza per eliminare i passaggi di consegna
options.es: Rediseño la secuencia para eliminar traspasos

options.en: Evaluate learning curve of alternative tools
options.it: Valuto la curva di apprendimento di strumenti alternativi
options.es: Evalúo la curva de aprendizaje de herramientas alternativas

options.en: Assess cost and licensing of replacements
options.it: Valuto costi e licenze dei sostituti
options.es: Evalúo costos y licencias de reemplazos

options.en: Trace each error back to the step where it first appears
options.it: Risalgo ogni errore al passaggio in cui compare per primo
options.es: Rastreo cada error hasta el paso donde aparece por primera vez

scoring_logic.formula: comp_power_score = mean(q1, q6, q11, q16, q21) / 3 * 100

monitoring_plan: Track q16 science_tech D option frequency with real data — target <30%
Monitor CP–Knowledge correlation with N≥500 real responses — alert if >0.50
Compute IRT difficulty parameters after N≥300 to validate ordinal scoring assumption
Review arts_creative q16 new stem engagement — confirm bottleneck scenario resonates
